title: Understanding the Pythagorean Theorem
text: The Pythagorean Theorem is a fundamental principle in geometry that relates the lengths of the sides of a right triangle. It states that the square of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

title: Identifying the Sides
text: In a right triangle, the hypotenuse is the longest side. The other two sides are called the legs. In this problem, the legs are $$3$$ cm and $$4$$ cm.

title: Applying the Pythagorean Theorem
text: Use the formula $$a^2+b^2=c^2$$. Substitute the given values: $$3^2+4^2=c^2$$.

title: Calculating the Squares
text: Square each leg and rewrite the equation: $$3^2+4^2=c^2$$ becomes $$9+16=c^2$$.

title: Summing the Squares
text: Add the squared values to get $$c^2=25$$.

title: Finding the Hypotenuse
text: Take the square root of the sum to find the hypotenuse: $$\sqrt{25}$$.

title: Understanding the Pythagorean Theorem
text: The Pythagorean Theorem is used to find the length of a side in a right triangle. It states that the square of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

title: Identifying the Sides
text: In this problem, the ladder acts as the hypotenuse of the right triangle. The distance from the wall (6 feet) and the height the ladder reaches on the wall (8 feet) are the other two sides.

title: Substitute the Values
text: Substitute the known values into the Pythagorean Theorem: $$6^2+8^2=c^2$$.

title: Calculate the Squares
text: Square the known side lengths and rewrite the equation: $$6^2+8^2=c^2$$ becomes $$36+64=c^2$$.
